root_text.an6.22:1.1: “Chayime, bhikkhave, aparihāniye dhamme desessāmi, taṁ suṇātha …pe…
root_text.an6.22:1.2: katame ca, bhikkhave, cha aparihāniyā dhammā?
root_text.an6.22:1.3: Na kammārāmatā, na bhassārāmatā, na niddārāmatā, na saṅgaṇikārāmatā, sovacassatā, kalyāṇamittatā—
root_text.an6.22:1.4: ime kho, bhikkhave, cha aparihāniyā dhammā.
root_text.an6.22:2.1: Ye hi keci, bhikkhave, atītamaddhānaṁ na parihāyiṁsu kusalehi dhammehi, sabbete imeheva chahi dhammehi na parihāyiṁsu kusalehi dhammehi.
root_text.an6.22:2.2: Yepi hi keci, bhikkhave, anāgatamaddhānaṁ na parihāyissanti kusalehi dhammehi, sabbete imeheva chahi dhammehi na parihāyissanti kusalehi dhammehi.
root_text.an6.22:2.3: Yepi hi keci, bhikkhave, etarahi na parihāyanti kusalehi dhammehi, sabbete imeheva chahi dhammehi na parihāyanti kusalehi dhammehī”ti.

translation_text.an6.22:1.1: “Mendicants, I will teach you these six principles that prevent decline. …
translation_text.an6.22:1.2: And what, mendicants, are the six principles that prevent decline?
translation_text.an6.22:1.3: Not relishing work, talk, sleep, and company, being easy to admonish, and having good friends.
translation_text.an6.22:2.1: Whether in the past, future, or present, all those who have not declined in skillful qualities do so because of these six qualities.”
